results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PROJECT AIMS TO DEVELOP A NOVEL TECHNOLOGY THAT WILL PREVENT HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS IN WATERING PONDS. THE RESEARCH INVOLVES THE SYNTHESIS OF EIGHT CANDIDATE MAGNESIUM-ALUMINUM OXIDE AND MAGNESIUM-IRON OXIDE SORBENT MEDIAS FOR THE UPTAKE OF UREA, AMMONIUM, PHOSPHATE, AND NITRATE IONS. THE TECHNOLOGY WILL ALLOW FOR THE ABSORBED NUTRIENTS TO BE USED AS A TIME-RELEASE FERTILIZER. THE GOAL WILL BE TO CREATE A SUSTAINABLE SOLUTION FOR THE PREVENTION OF HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS AND REDUCE THE ENVIRONMENTAL AND FINANCIAL COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH FERTILIZER PRODUCTION AND DECONTAMINATION OF PONDS.ACTIVITIES:THIS PROJECT CONDUCT AND PROMOTE THE COORDINATION AND ACCELERATION OF, RESEARCH, INVESTIGATIONS, EXPERIMENTS, TRAINING, DEMONSTRATIONS, SURVEYS, AND STUDIES RELATING TO THE CAUSES, EFFECTS, EXTENT, PREVENTION, REDUCTION, AND ELIMINATION OF POLLUTION. SPECIFICALLY, TO PREVENT EUTROPHICATION AND BE APPLIED AS A TIME-RELEASE FERTILIZER. THIS WILL IMPROVE THE UNDERSTANDING OF ENVIRONMENTAL DRIVERS OF HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS AND CONTRIBUTE TO THE UNDERSTANDING OF THE CHEMICAL AND THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP A HIGHLY SCALABLE, LOW-COST SORBENT MEDIA THAT WILL EFFICIENTLY CAPTURE EXCESS PLANT NUTRIENTS BIOLOGICAL INTEGRITY OF THE UPPER OHIO RIVER BASIN. BY UNDERSTANDING AND PREDICTING HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS, THIS PROJECT WILL ENABLE STATE AND LOCAL RESOURCE MANAGERS TO REDUCE THEIR OCCURRENCE AND THEREBY CONTRIBUTE TO THE PROTECTION OF CLEAN WATERSUBRECIPIENT:OUTCOMES:THE PROJECT AIMS TO DEVELOP A NOVEL TECHNOLOGY THAT WILL PREVENT HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS IN WATERING PONDS. THE RESEARCH INVOLVES THE SYNTHESIS OF EIGHT CANDIDATE MAGNESIUM-ALUMINUM OXIDE AND MAGNESIUM-IRON OXIDE SORBENT MEDIAS FOR THE UPTAKE OF UREA, AMMONIUM, PHOSPHATE, AND NITRATE IONS. THE TECHNOLOGY WILL ALLOW FOR THE ABSORBED NUTRIENTS TO BE USED AS A TIME-RELEASE FERTILIZER. THE GOAL WILL BE TO CREATE A SUSTAINABLE SOLUTION FOR THE PREVENTION OF HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS AND REDUCE THE ENVIRONMENTAL AND FINANCIAL COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH FERTILIZER PRODUCTION AND DECONTAMINATION OF PONDS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE THE GENERAL PUBLIC, SATE AND LOCAL REGULATORS, PUBLIC HEALTH DEPARTMENTS, WATER UTILITIES, AND COMMUNITIES IMPACTED BY DIFFERING SOURCES OF PFASS.

DESCRIPTION:
THE PROJECT AIMS TO DEVELOP A NOVEL TECHNOLOGY THAT WILL PREVENT HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS IN WATERING PONDS. THE RESEARCH INVOLVES THE SYNTHESIS OF EIGHT CANDIDATE MAGNESIUM-ALUMINUM OXIDE AND MAGNESIUM-IRON OXIDE SORBENT MEDIAS FOR THE UPTAKE OF UREA, AMMONIUM, PHOSPHATE, AND NITRATE IONS. THE TECHNOLOGY WILL ALLOW FOR THE ABSORBED NUTRIENTS TO BE USED AS A TIME-RELEASE FERTILIZER. THE GOAL WILL BE TO CREATE A SUSTAINABLE SOLUTION FOR THE PREVENTION OF HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS AND REDUCE THE ENVIRONMENTAL AND FINANCIAL COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH FERTILIZER PRODUCTION AND DECONTAMINATION OF PONDS.
ACTIVITIES:
THIS PROJECT CONDUCT AND PROMOTE THE COORDINATION AND ACCELERATION OF, RESEARCH, INVESTIGATIONS, EXPERIMENTS, TRAINING, DEMONSTRATIONS, SURVEYS, AND STUDIES RELATING TO THE CAUSES, EFFECTS, EXTENT, PREVENTION, REDUCTION, AND ELIMINATION OF POLLUTION. SPECIFICALLY, TO PREVENT EUTROPHICATION AND BE APPLIED AS A TIME-RELEASE FERTILIZER. THIS WILL IMPROVE THE UNDERSTANDING OF ENVIRONMENTAL DRIVERS OF HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS AND CONTRIBUTE TO THE UNDERSTANDING OF THE CHEMICAL AND THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP A HIGHLY SCALABLE, LOW-COST SORBENT MEDIA THAT WILL EFFICIENTLY CAPTURE EXCESS PLANT NUTRIENTS BIOLOGICAL INTEGRITY OF THE UPPER OHIO RIVER BASIN. BY UNDERSTANDING AND PREDICTING HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS, THIS PROJECT WILL ENABLE STATE AND LOCAL RESOURCE MANAGERS TO REDUCE THEIR OCCURRENCE AND THEREBY CONTRIBUTE TO THE PROTECTION OF CLEAN WATER
